_comment_1: Copy this file to user_config.json and edit it. ApplyCraft will read user_config.json on launch. The first-run setup wizard inside the app can also write this file for you.
name: Jane Doe
default_summary: Data Analytics professional with experience across [industries], specialising in turning complex data into decision-ready reporting. Skilled in Python, SQL, and modern BI tools, with a track record of automating workflows and driving adoption with non-technical stakeholders.
default_cover_letter_body:
At scale, the hardest part of analytics is not the analysis itself; it is deciding which signals deserve attention and how findings should be framed so leaders actually act. What drew me to this role at [Company Name] is that it treats data as a system with ownership and consequences, not as a reporting cycle.

In my work, I have spent much of my time operating in the gap between data and decision, clarifying intent upfront, stress-testing findings, and narrowing focus to actions that are both realistic and measurable.

I am applying because this role sits where analytical judgment, communication, and organisational responsibility intersect. I would value the opportunity to contribute to [Company Name]'s mission.
relocation_line: EU citizen, no visa required to work in EU. Currently in {location}, willing to relocate.
_comment_2: job_positions is the master inventory the CV builder reads. Each key is the heading shown in the document; the array is the default set of bullets for that role. You can have as many roles as you like.

ACME CORPORATION – Senior Data Analyst: Owned the analytics roadmap for a 40-person product team, translating ambiguous questions into shipped dashboards used in weekly business reviews.
Built and maintained the company-wide KPI layer in dbt + Snowflake, cutting time-to-insight for new reports from days to hours.
Mentored two junior analysts through structured code review and weekly 1:1s, both of whom were promoted within the year.
Partnered with engineering to instrument event tracking across web and mobile, increasing event coverage from 60% to 95% of critical flows.
Replaced an ageing Excel reporting pack with a Power BI suite adopted by Finance, Operations, and the CEO's office.
BETA RESEARCH GROUP – Data Scientist: Delivered analytical outputs across multiple projects, ensuring datasets were accurate, compliant, and usable by stakeholders.
Built a Python-based data quality tool that reduced manual review time by 80%, accelerating project delivery.
Produced dashboards and analytical summaries that enabled stakeholders to interpret sensitive data with confidence.
Presented findings to mixed technical and non-technical audiences, supporting informed project decisions.

_comment_3: templates maps a friendly label (shown in the UI) to the path of a .docx template. Paths can be absolute, or relative to the project root.
_comment_4: country_keywords lets stats_manager auto-tag the country of an application from the output filename. Add any city/country pairs you commonly target.
_comment_5: JD-aware bullet ranking uses one of four backends. ALL FOUR can run with zero data leaving your machine — only 'openai' makes a network call.
_comment_5a: 'local' (default) — pure-Python TF-IDF + keyword overlap. No install, instant, decent. Set this and you're done.
_comment_5b: 'sentence_transformers' — runs a small embedding model on-device (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 by default, ~80MB, MIT). pip install sentence-transformers. First run downloads weights; offline after that. Best balance of quality vs setup.
_comment_5c: 'ollama' — calls an Ollama daemon on localhost. Install Ollama (https://ollama.com), then `ollama pull nomic-embed-text`. Highest-quality local option if you already use Ollama.
_comment_5d: 'openai' — paid cloud API. Highest quality, but your JD text is sent to OpenAI. Opt-in only. Set provider='openai' AND api_key.
